mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding real output and corporate earnings capacity, which equity markets price in with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever INDPRO trend is positive.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO momentum periods show no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption slowdowns and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered-return does not differ significantly (p > 0.05) from unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated relative to trailing CPI inflation (implying a high real policy rate), tightening financial conditions compress equity valuations; the joint state of high FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL growth produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: real policy rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL inflation) > 1.5pp
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint real-rate interaction term with 20d forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative real-rate regime periods do not show significantly lower returns than positive real-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-sector recession regime, defined by housing starts remaining below their 5-year rolling average, further declines in HOUST amplify negative wealth-effect and construction-employment spillovers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that are stronger than in normal regimes.
failure_condition: In the housing-recession regime, 60-day forward SPX returns following further HOUST declines are not significantly more negative than returns during non-recession HOUST declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment is a fast-moving coincident indicator of economic momentum; persistent positive PAYEMS growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to incrementally improving growth expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change with 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign hit-rate is not significantly above 50% (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month retail sales growth accelerates beyond a threshold of 0.5%, it signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered RSAFS acceleration events fail to produce mean 20-day forward returns significantly greater than unconditional mean (p > 0.05) across at least two independent 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Labor market slack, measured as the joint occurrence of rising UNRATE and stagnant or declining PAYEMS growth, indicates a self-reinforcing deterioration in employment conditions that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter more strongly than either series predicts in isolation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising for 2 consecutive months AND PAYEMS month-over-month change <= 0
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction signal's Rank IC with 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or fails to outperform the single-series UNRATE-only signal in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound or near-zero policy rate regime, defined as FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, further monetary policy signals lose incremental transmission power, so short-horizon S&P 500 returns become insensitive to small FEDFUNDS changes; in this regime FEDFUNDS-based directional bets show no reliable edge, contrasting with normal regimes where rate cuts drive positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: If within the FEDFUNDS<0.5% regime, 5-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS declines are statistically indistinguishable from returns following FEDFUNDS increases (p > 0.05), the zero-lower-bound insensitivity claim is falsified.
